title: Big Mom’s Broken Memory
description: On pages 7–10, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Big Mom, Giants, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #7, Charlotte Linlin as Big Mom’s past explains the emotional weakness that the assassination plan tries to exploit.
scene: Big Mom’s childhood reveals the loss and confusion behind her obsession with Mother Carmel.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: And on the island

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: You'll be okay now, Linlin!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Come be friends with all of us, until your mother and father come back!

dialogue.speaker: Giants
dialogue.text: that the Giants call home, Elbaf,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: 37 years since that day, ''The Lamb's house.''

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: They ain't comin' back, Idiot!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: She devoted her life to the building of a bridge of friendship between man and giant.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They dumped you here like a load of garbage!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Everyone here's a bit unique, but...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Unique enough for their parents to not want 'em, hahaha!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You should be able to befriend them in no time!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: mother caramel (80 years old) and the children of the lamb's house

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Okay! Thanks!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I'm so hungry!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: You're freakin' huge!

dialogue.speaker: Giants
dialogue.text: Are you a giant or some- thin'?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Well, let's have some food, then!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A prince driven out of his country by riots and revolutions.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: children forced into near-slavery by poverty.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: problem children recalcitrant beyond even the patience of their parents.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Here these young ones could live amongst the giants, unfettered by status or mileage.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: A new girl?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But above all, Linlin, who had spent her whole life being bigger than everyone around her,

dialogue.speaker: Giants
dialogue.text: First saw in Elbaf

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: whoaaa

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: people, houses, and scenery that seemed made for her size.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: It was an indescribably comforting feeling.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: WHY...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: WHY...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: WAAA AHAH!

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: OH NO! WHAT'S THE MATTER, LINLIN?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Mister bear and mister wolf were fighting in the forest!

dialogue.speaker: Charlotte Linlin
dialogue.text: Mother! You know,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And because it's not good to fight, I decided I needed to make them be friends!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So I put them in the same cage!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But that silly mister bear ate mister wolf!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And because he was being bad, I gave Mister Bear a spanky!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Looks like Mister Bear is thinking hard about what he did wrong. How many times did you spank him?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Just once!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: She killed it instantly...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You wanted both of them to be friends, didn't you?

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: You're such a kind girl, Linlin!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Yes!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: of all the people Linlin had met in her life,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This was the first that actually understood her.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: I saw it!

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: Linlin killed that bear in one hit!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: She's crazy!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Did you hear? She got in a huge fight with Eyri!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Apparently there was a mosquito on him, so she tried to smack it!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: She broke a bunch of his bones!

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: What are you doing, Linlin?!

dialogue.speaker: Charlotte Linlin
dialogue.text: GYAAAH! Save me, Mother!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He has one extra joint, so I thought I'd tear that extra bit off for him!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No! He's from the Longarm Tribe! That's how he's supposed to be!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He has fins, so I thought I'd just rip them off for him!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No! The Merpeople look like fish! That's how they're supposed to be!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Everyone! Forgive her! Linlin is a kind girl!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: caramel forgave everything she did.

summary: This section advances the Whole Cake Island Tea Party storyline with 59 dialogue beats. Big Mom’s past reframes her weakness and the emotional core of the failed assassination plan.
